LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS YOSEF PERETZ AND SUMY KIM'S MOTION FOR ANTI-SLAPP ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. MR. PERETZ AND MS. KIM ARE ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,550 AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,154 AGAINST PLAINTIFF KAZIM ACAR. AS PREVAILING PARTIES ON AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, MR. PERETZ AND MS. KIM ARE ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF THEIR REASONABLY INCURRED FEES AND COSTS. THEY PROVIDED AMPLE EVIDENCE THAT THE AMOUNTS THEY SEEK WERE REASONABLY INCURRED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS YOSEF PERETZ, SUMY KIM, JENNIFER CRANE AND FAHIMEH RAHRAVAN'S MOTION TO DECLARE PLAINTIFF KAZIM ACAR A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AND TO PROHIBIT HIM FROM FILING ANY NEW LITIGATION WITHOUT BEING REPRESENTED BY COUNSEL IN ANY CALIFORNIA COURT ABSENT OBTAINING LEAVE OF COURT FROM THE PRESIDING JUSTICE OR PRESIDING JUDGE IS GRANTED. THE MOVING PARTIES HAVE SHOWN THAT MR. ACAR IS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AS THAT TERM IS DEFINED IN CCP 391(A)(1) BECAUSE HE HAS COMMENCED AT LEAST FIVE LITIGATIONS OTHER THAN IN SMALL CLAIMS COURT THAT HAVE BEEN FINALLY DETERMINED ADVERSELY TO HIM. THE MOVING PARTIES HAVE ALSO PROVIDED AMPLE EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THEIR REQUEST PER CCP 391.7 THAT MR. ACAR SHOULD NOT BE ALLOWED TO FILE ANY NEW LITIGATION WITHOUT BEING REPRESENTED BY COUNSEL IN ANY CALIFORNIA COURT ABSENT OBTAINING LEAVE OF COURT FROM THE PRESIDING JUSTICE OR PRESIDING JUDGE OF THE COURT WHERE HE SEEKS TO FILE THE NEW LITIGATION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS YOSEF PERETZ'S AND SUMY KIM'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Defendants Jennifer E. Cranes And Fahimeh Rahravans Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Sumy Kim of the law offices of Peretz & Associates (415.732.3777, ext. 105) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Jennifer Crane and Fahimeh Rahravan's motion for anti-SLAPP attorney's fees and costs is GRANTED. Ms. Crane and Ms. Rahravan are entitled to an award of fees in the amount of $3,910 and costs in the amount of $1,154 against plaintiff Kazim Acar. As prevailing parties on an anti-SLAPP motion, Ms. Crane and Ms. Rahravan are entitled to an award of their reasonably incurred fees and costs. They provided ample evidence that the amounts they seek were reasonably incurred. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Defendants Motion For A Prefiling Order Prohibiting Vexatious Litigant Kazim Z. Acar From Filing New Litigation In Propria Persona
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Sumy Kim of the law offices of Peretz & Associates (415.732.3777, ext. 105) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Yosef Peretz, Sumy Kim, Jennifer Crane and Fahimeh Rahravan's motion to declare plaintiff Kazim Acar a vexatious litigant and to prohibit him from filing any new litigation without being represented by counsel in any California court absent obtaining leave of court from the presiding justice or presiding judge is GRANTED. The moving parties have shown that Mr. Acar is a vexatious litigant as that term is defined in CCP 391(a)(1) because he has commenced at least five litigations other than in small claims court that have been finally determined adversely to him. The moving parties have also provided ample evidence to support their request per CCP 391.7 that Mr. Acar should not be allowed to file any new litigation without being represented by counsel in any California court absent obtaining leave of court from the presiding justice or presiding judge of the court where he seeks to file the new litigation. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

They provided ample evidence that the amounts they seek were reasonably incurred. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
